title: salix × pedunculata
intro: Salix × pedunculata, also known as the willow-leaved willow or weeping willow, is a hybrid of the Salix alba (white willow) and Salix fragilis (crack willow) plants. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects due to the presence of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × pedunculata include relief from pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as potential antiseptic and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
